items.reason: All 20 decisions (DEC-001 through DEC-020) are documented in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to populated. The five new platform-shape decisions (DEC-016 dictionary direction, DEC-017 canonical fields, DEC-018 enums, DEC-019 privacy classes, DEC-020 migration graph) are present. `decisions-needed.md` enumerates each required area and maps it to a decision id.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-15, blocker product priority) and PEND-005 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-03, blocker CI capacity), each with a one-line rationale. Both target dates are in the future relative to the 2026-04-26 research date.

Each decision's consequences enumerates concrete artifacts, code paths, or categories that the decision deletes. E.g., DEC-001 names removal of subject-to-person merge branches in specific Edge Function files; DEC-007 removes duplicate source-ID columns naming `public.source_identifiers`; DEC-016 removes hand-edited per-spec dictionaries. The 'why not collapse' justifications also defend against a more aggressive simplification.

Each decision lists three credible architectural alternatives spanning aggressive simplification, permissive preservation, and the chosen middle. E.g., DEC-005 considers AGS-as-canonical (aggressive collapse), peer source-native stores (permissive), and the chosen current-state plus history. These are real choices production platforms make, not strawmen.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` reads `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` (568 fields across 5 specs per projection_summary) and emits the per-spec `dictionary/*.v1.json` files. The script supports `--check` mode and is invoked by `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py`, which calls it via subprocess and fails on drift. VERIFY.md runs `--check` first.

The `canonical_field_id` values in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` are namespaced per-spec (e.g. `canonical.oneroster-core.person.email`, not a single shared id). DEC-017 requires that overlapping fields like OneRoster `User.email`, Caliper `Person.email`, LTI `lis_person_contact_email_primary`, and QTI `Candidate.email` resolve to ONE canonical field — but the email-shaped fields in different specs each get their own per-spec canonical id, so cross-spec overlap is not resolved at the data level. Additionally, the per-spec projections in `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` (shown spec_field blocks) do not carry `canonical_field_id` at all.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: replace per-spec canonical_field_id namespaces with one canonical identity (e.g., `canonical.identity.email`) referenced by every overlapping spec field, AND propagate canonical_field_id into the projected dictionary/*.v1.json field records via scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py.

items.reason: There is no single canonical identity object in the shared dictionary. The seed gives each spec object its own `canonical_object_id` (`canonical.oneroster-core.person`, `canonical.caliper-core.caliper_actor`, etc.). DEC-001 prose declares a canonical Person/Agent split, but no canonical identity entity record with its own fields, allowed values, and projections exists in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json`. The unification remains prose-only.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a top-level canonical identity object (e.g., `canonical.identity.person`) with canonical fields and have every spec-side identity object (Person, User, Actor, Subject, Candidate, lti_membership) reference it via canonical_object_id.

items.reason: Per-spec enums are declared inline in each per-spec dictionary rather than referencing a shared enum table with bidirectional crosswalks. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.primary_role` lists `student/teacher/administrator/guardian` inline; the LTI roles in `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_launch.roles` reference URI-style values; there is no shared `role_family` enum mapping spec-native values to one canonical vocabulary. The only shared enum reference observed is `base_status` for active/inactive/tobedeleted, which doesn't span specs.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add `shared_enums` with role_family, organization_type, status, score_status, etc., each mapping spec-native values to canonical values bidirectionally; replace inline allowed_values in per-spec projections with `shared_enum_id` references via scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py.

items.reason: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` defines a closed nine-value privacy class list (public, directory, education_record, sensitive, credential, behavioral, operational, privacy_governance, system). `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` validates that every object/field uses one of these values and rejects `depends_on_contents` / `depends_on_entity` placeholders explicitly via `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES`. DEC-019 codifies the closure.

The shared seed dictionary objects do not carry an explicit `relationships` array declaring FK targets, cardinality, and ownership. Foreign keys live in hand-written `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` (e.g. `references public.organizations(id)`) and there is no generator that produces these migrations from the dictionary graph. DEC-020 declares the intent but the implementation is absent — VERIFY.md does not run a migration generator and there's no `scripts/generate_migrations.py`.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add per-object `relationships` (target object, cardinality, on_delete) AND create scripts/generate_migrations.py that emits supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql; wire it into VERIFY.md with --check drift detection.

items.reason: Every field, object, and controlled value in the per-spec dictionaries carries a `decision_id` referencing decisions in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`. `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates that every field's `decision_id` exists in the decision trace block AND that the field appears in that decision's `produces_fields`, failing on broken links.

Generators exist (`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, `generate_caliper_core.py`, `generate_integration_governance_core.py`) and produce Markdown + HTML dictionary pages from the structured sources. `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` verifies field/value coverage in artifacts. However, hand-edited prose pages like `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` and `docs/spec-gap-backlog.md` make many load-bearing claims (e.g. specific field names, deferred items) without anchored links to dictionary entries. `site/index.html` describes API groups in prose without per-claim dictionary anchors.
items.traced_cause: scripts/build_site_docs.py and docs/lead-spec-accounting.md: add anchor links from every load-bearing field/object claim in prose docs to its generated dictionary entry, and add a link-check step to VERIFY.md that fails on dictionary anchors that don't resolve.

items.reason: The decision register `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` is rendered to HTML and linked from the portal (`docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html`). DEC entries explain choice and simplification clearly. However, the rubric requires that each concept-introducing doc page reference its decision in plain language, not just link to a 900-line raw register. The portal's decision section says 'Each card is rendered from the canonical decision register' but there's no evidence each concept (e.g., why enrollment vs membership, why CASE is canonical) gets its own plain-language explanation in concept docs near where the concept is introduced.
items.traced_cause: docs/concepts/*.md (or equivalent): add a short plain-language 'why this exists' section per major concept (person/agent split, enrollment vs membership, CASE canonical, etc.) that quotes the decision's choice and links to its anchor in docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md.

items.reason: `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` does not exist. README.md lists many doc artifacts but no buildability guide. The portal `site/index.html` has a 'Build an app' button linking to a #guide section that contains generic 'Platform Provides / App Developer Owns' bullets, not a copy-pasteable end-to-end walkthrough of (a) creating a school, (b) enrolling students, (c) posting a grade, (d) linking to CASE, (e) reading Caliper feed.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: create the file with five copy-pasteable curl/SQL steps against live Supabase REST + Edge Functions, with each field linked to its dictionary entry and each enum value linked to its global enum.

items.reason: No evidence of a link checker run over the rendered site or its result. VERIFY.md runs JSON formatters and dictionary artifact checks but does not include a link-checker step. The portal landing reaches generated dictionary pages, decision register, and OpenAPI pages, but reachability of every dictionary object and decision within three clicks is not verified. The backlog item 25 explicitly notes 'duplicate or orphaned HTML' from generators is unresolved.
items.traced_cause: VERIFY.md and scripts/check_site_links.py: add a deterministic link checker that fails on 404s and orphan pages, and resolve the duplicate generated dictionary HTML pages noted in spec-gap-backlog.md item 25.

items.reason: No buildability guide exists; a layperson reading only `docs/` cannot produce executable artifacts for the five required steps. Beyond the missing guide, even with the existing docs the developer would hit substantive gaps: cross-spec field reconciliation isn't unified (canonical_field_id is per-spec, not shared), there's no shared enum crosswalk for roles, no unified canonical identity entity, and no documented endpoint for creating schools/classes (existing Edge Functions cover bulk-submit grades, audited reads, LTI launch receipts, Caliper ingestion, OAuth exchange — none POST schools or enrollments).
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus the cross-spec dictionary unification (shared canonical_field_id, shared canonical identity, shared role enum) and a documented org/class/enrollment write path: write the guide, fix the data-level overlaps, and add or document the create endpoints.

items.reason: `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` enforces non-empty `traced_cause` on every non-pass item via `ensure_traced_causes()` and `normalize_traced_cause()`, with per-item DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES fallbacks. It also normalizes a `buildability_gaps` array with traced_cause per gap via `normalize_buildability_gaps()`. The output schema is enforced in code.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. `scripts/codex_loop.py` invokes it via `run_evaluator()` both before and after each iteration. The evaluator writes the report with `done`, per-item status, traced_cause, and buildability_gaps.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` reading `docs/loop-overrides.md`, `overrides_paused()` checking for the literal PAUSE token on its own line (ignoring HTML comments), invokes it BEFORE each iteration runs, and `build_prompt()` injects formatted overrides under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `before_eval.get('done')` before starting work and `after_eval.get('done')` after each iteration, returning 0 when done is true. It also has a stall detector (--stall-limit) and rubric-progressed gating for publish. The hard cap (--max-iterations) is a safety rail, not a numeric-threshold gate.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented endpoint or guide step for creating a school org with a teacher and ten students. The only write-capable Edge Functions are gradebook-bulk-submit, audited-roster-read, lti-launch-handler, caliper-event-ingestion, and oauth-token-exchange — none POST organizations, people, or enrollments.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus a new supabase/functions/roster-bulk-upsert/index.ts (or documented Supabase REST insert path with the required tenant_id, sourced_id, and identifier-crosswalk fields): create the guide step and the create endpoint.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented endpoint to create a class with course_id/term_id/school_id linkage and enroll the ten students. The OneRoster dictionary describes the schema but the runtime exposes only read paths for these tables.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and supabase/functions/* (or documented PostgREST insert with required RLS-tenant-claim headers): document the class+enrollment create flow with copy-pasteable curl referencing dictionary anchors for class.title, class.course_id, enrollment.role, etc.

buildability_gaps.gap: While `gradebook-bulk-submit` exists with a curl example in `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md`, the layperson guide does not link `scoreStatus` enum values to the dictionary's global enum (none exists yet) and does not show how to create the prerequisite line_item.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus a shared scoreStatus enum in data/data-dictionary.seed.json with shared_enum_id: prepend a step to create the line_item via documented insert path, link scoreStatus values to the shared enum, and show the full grade-post flow end-to-end.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented runtime path links an assignment (line_item) to a CASE standard URI. The CASE dictionary is doc-only per DEC-012, and there is no alignment table or Edge Function that records 'line_item X aligns to CASE item Y'.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json (add line_item.alignment_target_uri or a line_item_alignment object with FK to case_item) and a supabase/migrations migration plus an Edge Function or PostgREST write path; documented in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md.

buildability_gaps.gap: `caliper-event-ingestion` writes audit_log receipts but does not project events into a queryable feed, and no documented endpoint returns 'all Caliper events for class X'. The Caliper dictionary describes caliper_events tables that have no migrations.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/migrations/* (generate caliper_events / caliper_envelopes tables from data/data-dictionary.seed.json relationships) and a supabase/functions/caliper-event-feed Edge Function or PostgREST view; documented in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md.

buildability_gaps.gap: Across all five steps the developer would have to reconcile spec-native field names manually because canonical_field_id is per-spec namespaced (`canonical.oneroster-core.person.email` vs `canonical.caliper-core.caliper_actor.identifier_value`), not unified to one shared canonical email/identity field.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: introduce shared canonical objects/fields (e.g., `canonical.identity.person`, `canonical.identity.email`) and re-key per-spec canonical_field_id values to point to them, propagating into per-spec projections via scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
